evidence: File begins with '# Active Directory Penetration Testing Plugin' — no YAML frontmatter block at all; applies uniformly across all 56 command files
description: All 56 command files lack description: frontmatter, causing blank entries in Claude Code /help listing and reducing discoverability
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter with description: field to each command file; e.g. '---\ndescription: "Active Directory penetration testing — enumeration, credential attacks, and hardening"\n---'

evidence: No YAML frontmatter present in any of the 56 command files; confirmed by reading every file — none contain allowed-tools: field
description: All 56 command files lack allowed-tools: frontmatter declaration; tool constraints are undeclared, weakening least-privilege posture for read-only advisor commands
suggested_fix: Add allowed-tools: list in frontmatter for each command; read-only advisors (threat-modeler, hallucination-guard, etc.) should restrict to [Read, Glob, Grep]

evidence: File ends with 'Test Active Directory: $ARGUMENTS' with no conditional fallback; same pattern confirmed in all 56 files
description: No command handles empty $ARGUMENTS; invoking any command without arguments produces no usage hint or example
suggested_fix: Prepend each command's invocation line with: 'If no arguments provided, show 2-3 example invocations and a brief capability summary.'

evidence: File has ## Core Functions, ## Commands, ## Rules sections but no ## Output Format section; ends with 'Execute memory operation: $ARGUMENTS'
description: memory-vault.md lacks an output format template, leaving callers unable to anticipate what a save/recall/list operation will produce
suggested_fix: Add ## Output Format section with concrete templates for each sub-command (save, recall, list, clean, export, search)

evidence: 'properly' appears 4 times: 'Session tokens cryptographically random' context — 'Properly random'; 'Role-based or attribute-based'; 'Properly managed'; 'Environment-specific settings properly managed'
description: secure-code-review.md uses 'properly' as an unmeasured qualifier 4 times; reviewers cannot determine compliance without concrete criteria
suggested_fix: Replace 'properly hashed' with 'hashed with bcrypt cost≥12 or Argon2id'; replace 'properly managed' with 'rotated every 90 days via secrets manager'

evidence: 'unnecessary' appears 4 times: 'Remove unnecessary roles and features', 'Disable unnecessary services', 'Remove unnecessary packages', and in Unnecessary HTTP methods enabled under API8
description: blue-team-hardening.md uses 'unnecessary' 4 times without defining what qualifies as unnecessary; different implementors will reach different conclusions
suggested_fix: Reference a specific baseline such as CIS Benchmark Level 1 control IDs instead of 'unnecessary'; list specific services to disable

evidence: 'relevant' appears 3 times ('relevant information is loaded', 'Rank information by relevance', 'files that are likely to be affected') and 'intelligently' appears once ('intelligently gather context')
description: context-manager.md uses 'relevant' and 'intelligently' without operational definitions, making the prioritisation heuristics opaque
suggested_fix: Replace 'relevant information' with explicit Priority 1-4 tiers (already partially done); replace 'intelligently' with 'using the Priority 1-4 tiers defined below'

evidence: 'proper' in 'Proper secret injection' and 'unnecessary' in 'Unnecessary features/endpoints disabled'
description: devsecops.md uses 'proper' and 'unnecessary' without specifying tools or criteria
suggested_fix: Replace 'proper secret injection' with 'External Secrets Operator or Vault Agent sidecar'; replace 'unnecessary features' with a reference list of endpoints to disable

evidence: 'unnecessary' in 'No unnecessary dependencies' and 'proper' in 'Proper secrets management' and 'from trusted sources'
description: supply-chain-sec.md uses 'unnecessary', 'proper', and 'trusted' without measurable criteria for each
suggested_fix: Define 'unnecessary dependency' as any direct dep with zero code references after treeshake; define 'trusted' as packages with SLSA L2+ provenance

evidence: 'realistic attack scenarios' line 18 and 'proper scope and rules of engagement' line 8 in authorization section
description: red-team-ops.md uses 'realistic' and 'proper' without defining what realistic ATT&CK coverage or proper scope documentation requires
suggested_fix: Replace 'realistic' with '≥5 ATT&CK tactics covered across kill-chain'; replace 'proper scope' with 'signed rules-of-engagement document'

evidence: Line 134: cp "$cmd_file" "$CLAUDE_COMMANDS_DIR/$filename" where CLAUDE_COMMANDS_DIR expands to ~/.claude/commands/ outside the repo
description: install.sh writes plugin files to ~/.claude/commands/ — outside the repository tree
fp_reason: Writing to ~/.claude/commands/ is the declared, documented purpose of this installer script; the destination is hard-coded and user-visible in the help text
rule_gap: SEC-file-write-outside-repo should skip files whose basename matches *install*.sh or *uninstall*.sh or whose help text advertises the write destination

evidence: Line 33: rm "$CLAUDE_COMMANDS_DIR/$filename" removes files from ~/.claude/commands/ outside the repo
description: uninstall.sh removes plugin files from ~/.claude/commands/ — outside the repository tree
fp_reason: Removing installed files from ~/.claude/commands/ is the declared, documented purpose of this uninstaller script
rule_gap: SEC-file-write-outside-repo should distinguish destructive installer removal (rm on own files) from backdoor file deletion

evidence: All 56 audited command files are self-contained; no cross-file references or shared utilities were observed
description: No cross-component issues found; all commands are appropriately independent with no broken references, stale counts, or terminology drift
